message: Trays still cooling from yesterday's batch. Cornbread base separated again - the heat's too aggressive or the ratio's drifting. We're fixing it today.
The separation thing - is it the egg ratio or are we overcooking the batter before it sets?
The separation's probably the batter setting before the crumb structure can hold it - that's where the story breaks down, literally.

Marcus is actually right - which annoys her because he usually just talks around the point. The batter needs to set gradually, not violently. She wants to acknowledge he nailed the diagnosis without making it a whole thing. It's the oven temperature. We're hitting 425 when we should be at 375 - gives the structure time to form before the edges char.
So 375 lets the cornbread actually breathe - that's the difference between a crumb that holds together and one that just fractures under its own weight.
okay so 375 and we let it actually set - that's our lock. good catch on the breathing thing, that's the piece that makes sense now. heading out, we've got what we need.
